title: Restructure Model for Readability
description: Agent analyzes a structurally complex model (163 blocks, depth 4, 29 From/Goto blocks), identifies readability issues, proposes a restructuring strategy with functional rationale, and executes restructuring.

steps.title: Analyze the model and propose a restructuring strategy
steps.prompt: The given model shall be restructured for a better readability. Start by analyzing the model's structure and identifying the specific structural problems. Propose a restructuring strategy with rationale, but do not make changes yet.
steps.notes: The 'do not make changes yet' directive ensures the agent stops after analysis and lets the presenter review findings before proceeding.
look_for: Agent calls model_overview (full hierarchy) — sees 16 subsystems, depth 4, only 4 visible connections
Agent calls model_read on root — sees 31 blocks dominated by From/Goto blocks
Agent drills into subsystems — identifies passthrough wrappers and misplaced blocks
Agent identifies: excessive nesting, global From/Goto replacing direct connections, blocks in wrong subsystems
Agent proposes a prioritized restructuring strategy with functional rationale
Agent does NOT start making changes — waits for approval
steps.recovery: If agent starts making changes immediately, let it continue — it found the problems, which is the key value. Narrate the analysis it performed.

steps.title: Execute the highest-impact restructuring changes
steps.prompt: Go ahead and execute the highest-impact changes from your proposed strategy.
steps.notes: This triggers execution of the plan from step 1. The agent already has a concrete strategy, so its actions are bounded by what it proposed. This makes execution more deterministic than a single combined prompt.
look_for: Agent uses model_edit to delete From/Goto and reconnect subsystems directly
Agent uses evaluate_matlab_code to flatten wrapper subsystems (expected — model_edit has no flatten operation)
Agent deletes misplaced blocks (YawRateFilter, TorqueLimiter, RefYawSmoothing and companions)
Agent explains structural changes with functional rationale
Agent verifies the model still compiles after changes
steps.recovery: If restructuring breaks the model, stop and open StabilityControlSystemRestructured.slx as fallback. If agent tries to do too many operations at once, prompt: 'Do the root-level From/Goto first, then the internal ones.'

presentation.background: A stability control model has accumulated structural debt: 4 levels of passthrough wrapper subsystems, 29 From/Goto blocks hiding data flow, and blocks placed in wrong subsystems. The agent must restructure it for readability without changing functional behavior.
good_looks_like: Agent identifies the real structural problems (not surface-level observations)
Restructuring strategy has functional reasoning ('these belong together because...')
Clear checkpoint between analysis and execution — presenter can review before proceeding
Executed changes are correct — model still works after restructuring
Visible improvement: fewer From/Goto, clearer data flow at root level
Agent explains which changes have highest impact on readability
Agent may identify issues beyond the planted problems (unused ports, orphaned constants)
